The following is a list of test cases for a metering system, as described in the input format. The purpose of each test case is indicated in the "Purpose" field. The "Test steps" field outlines the steps to follow when executing the test.

1. Test case for automatic collection of metered values: Verify that the metering system can be configured for regular automatic collection of metered values from Metering Units to HES.
2. Test case for security logs: Verify that all relevant information is logged in the security log.
3. Test case for protection of meters: Verify that the meter is protected against intrusion, tampering, and malware execution.
4. Test case for sending the same information twice: Verify that the system can send the same information twice, once from the file and once from the meter.
5. Test case for controlling logout time: Wait for 45 minutes.
6. Test case for developing test cases: The supplier shall prepare test cases based on agreed requirements.
7. Test case for training for UAT: The Contractor shall carry out the necessary training and submit the relevant documentation according to point 5 before the start of the Customer's UAT.
8. Test case for support under UAT: The supplier should be present at the purchaser's premises and assist when the purchaser performs UAT.
9. Test case for metering units in HES: Verify that it is possible to install new Metering Unit - Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2, Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2, Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2 in HES.
10. Test case for recording communication errors: Verify that the Metering System is able to record and store information when communication failures occur.
11. Test case for recording when high power exceeds limit value per phase: Verify that the Metering System is able to record and store information when power output exceeds the limit value per phase.
12. Test case for recording when power output exceeds limit value: Verify that the Metering System is able to record and store information when total power consumption exceeds the limit value.
13. Test case for blocking switches from HES: Verify that the Meter breaker can be blocked from HES to prevent local connection for Cat 1 - 1 phase meters, Cat 1 - 3 phase meters, and Cat 2 meters.
14. Test case for metering unit identification: Verify that the marking of the meter's manufacturing number is clearly legible and easily readable for customers.
15. Test case for life expectancy of communication equipment: Verify that communication equipment meets the life expectancy requirement of >=15 years from the date of delivery to GE.
16. Test case for design of the solution: Review installed solution and compare with requirements in 2.1 Technical solution.
17. Test case for logging events: Verify in HES that the events and their attribute values can be displayed in an event list.
18. Test case for installation of new metering device via integration: Verify that information regarding structure data for new set-up is sent from the integration interface Mint to Collection Manager. An acknowledgment is sent to Generis that the meter is set up in Collection Manager. Check that the user point is displayed under the correct coordinates in the map view in Vision. Check that the meter has the correct profile and that Energy values, PQ values, and GSM diagnostics are pushed from the meter at the correct interval.
19. Test case for dismantling of metering unit via integration: Information regarding structure data for the removal of a metering unit is sent from the integration interface Mint to Collection Manager. Verify that the metering unit is removed in Collection Manager and does not have a connection to the user point.
20. Test case for updating measurement point in CM: Information regarding the structure data for updating a metering unit is sent from the integration interface Mint to Collection Manager. Verify that the information to be updated via Mint has also been changed at the current user point.
21. Test case for new metering unit set up in CM and taken down in ACM: Initiate a new set-up of a metering unit in CM and ensure that the metering unit is still in ACM when the new set-up is carried out in CM. A message is sent from CM to Generis stating that the metering unit in ACM is not taken down.
22. Test case for new metering unit set up in CM and taken down in AiMiR: Initiate a new set-up of a metering unit in CM and ensure that the metering unit is still in AiMir when the new set-up is carried out in CM. A message is sent to the AiMir box.
23. Test case for existing metering unit change in CM: Initiate a meter change of an existing metering unit in CM to a New Metering Unit. Enter incorrect serial number for the existing meter in the Work Order. Message is sent from CM to Generis stating that the metering unit is not created in CM. Verify the status of the work order in Generis.
24. Test case for new metering unit set up in CM: Initiate a new installation of a metering unit in CM. Enter a serial number for the metering unit already connected to an installation in the work order. Message is sent from CM to Generis stating that the metering unit is not created in CM. Verify the status of the work order in Generis.
25. Test case for metering unit take-down in CM: Initiate a removal of a metering unit in CM where mandatory information in the work order is not complete. Message is sent from CM to Generis stating that the metering unit is not removed in CM. Verify the status of the work order in Generis.
26. Test case for hardware for the user interface: HES should be designed to be used with different types of computers, tablets and smart phones, with different architectures and operating systems.

These test cases cover various aspects of the metering system, including security, installation, and operation. It is important to thoroughly test the system to ensure that it meets the required specifications and functions correctly and efficiently.
